That part is also good because it stops us from saying we know something when we only have a true thought that happened by chance. But the

english_query: why following rules is not enough if you do not care about people
ugf_response: First, we think about what it means to follow a set of things we are told to do. These things help keep order. But they are just words on paper. They do not have feelings. They do not know if a person is sad or hurt. Next, we look at what it means to care for people. This means we want others to be happy and safe. We want to help them when they are in trouble. Now, imagine a thing we are told to do might say you must do one thing. But if you see someone in trouble, you might choose not to do it because the thing says so. You do not think about the hurt. You just want to follow the thing. This is bad because the thing is only words. It does not know every small thing that happens. It has no heart. If you only follow the words, you might do the right thing by the book, but you might still be mean or not nice. So, caring about people is more important than just doing what is said. If you do not care about people, you might use the words to hurt them. You might say that the thing is okay, even if it is not. This shows that caring is more important. It helps us do the right thing even when the things we are told to do are not clear or are not right. We need to use our
